L2N4-3QP: Mary Broughton was born in 1634 in Tur Langton, Leicestershire, England, and was baptized in November 1633 at Doncaster, Yorkshire. Like many of her generation, she crossed the Atlantic to the British colonies, and she died in 1695 in Scituate, Plymouth, in the Massachusetts Bay Colony.
MMJ4-YV1: Alice Agnes Gernelli Rycroft was born in 1645 in Settle, Yorkshire, England. A member of the Religious Society of Friends, she lived out her faith as a Quaker and eventually settled in Bucks, Pennsylvania, where she died in 1727. She was laid to rest in the Middletown Friends Cemetery.
LYLF-76P: William Brereton was born in 1636 at St Mary the Virgin's Church in Henbury, Gloucestershire, England, and made his home in Henbury near Bristol. At some point he left England for the colonies, and he died in 1690 in Somerset, Maryland.
LH7Z-X39: Margaret Low was born in 1632 in Boxford, Suffolk, England. She emigrated to New England and died in 1693 in Ipswich, Essex, in the Massachusetts Bay Colony.
LCX5-WKX: John Knight III was born in 1648 in Newbury, Essex, in the Massachusetts Bay Colony, and lived his whole life in that same community. He died there in 1723.
LVHF-KTP: Owen Griffith was born in 1624 in Penllech, Caernarfonshire, Wales. He immigrated to Virginia in 1666, where he rose to serve as the King's Attorney. He wrote his will in July 1698 and died that same year.
LZG5-YNZ: Mary Chase was born in 1685 in Tisbury, on the island of Dukes County in the New York Colony. She lived a remarkably long life, eventually settling in Carteret, North Carolina, where she died in 1788 at the age of around 103.
L876-55W: Ambrose G Bramlett was born in 1664 in Warwickshire, England, and arrived in America in April 1690 at Charles City, Virginia. He settled in Virginia and was among the early bearers of the Bramlett name in Bedford County. He died in 1732 in Bedford City, Virginia.
LHD8-FWH: Rachel McAllister was born in 1686 in Virginia and lived her life in the colony. She died in 1748 in Louisa, Virginia.
L2SD-DZG: Euclydus Longshore was born in 1692 in Philadelphia, Pennsylvania. By 1715 he had settled in Middletown Township, Bucks County, where he made his home for the rest of his life. He wrote his will in November 1760, and it was proved in October 1764, the year of his death in Langhorne, Middletown Township.
L2VR-5GZ: William Bramlett I was born in 1694 in Essex, Virginia. He first appears in the records in 1715 as a witness to a land sale in Essex, and by 1719 he held a plantation in Middlesex County. Over the following decades he moved through Caroline, Lunenburg, and finally Bedford County, where he died in 1762.
L5Y6-JBY: William Case was born in 1615 in Aylesham, Kent, England, and in 1635 sailed to the colonies as a passenger aboard the Dorset under master John Flower. He was made a freeman in 1655, and after moving from Boston to Rhode Island around 1665, he served the General Court of Newport as a deputy and later as a juryman. Possibly a Quaker, he sold his interests in Conanicut and Dutch Island and died in Newport in 1676.
GPFP-38T: Nicolas-Jacob Bourgeois, also known as Jacques and called dit Grandjehan, was born in 1575 in Laferté-sur-Amance in the Haute-Marne region of France. He is recorded as having made the voyage to Canada. He died in 1621 in Champagne-et-Fontaine, Dordogne, France.
L5F9-8J8: Margaret was born in 1611 in London, England, and made her way to colonial Virginia. In July 1653 she received a patent for 700 acres on the north side of the Rappahannock River in Lancaster County, granted for the transportation of thirteen persons. She died in 1665 in Old Rappahannock, Virginia.
LZ4V-L38: Nathaniel Haven was born in 1664 in Lynn, Essex, in the Massachusetts Bay Colony. He lived his life in Massachusetts and died in 1746 in Framingham, Middlesex County.
MYTB-RXD: John Johann Blume was born in 1742 in Denmark and made his way to America. He gave military service to the United States, with records placing him in arms in January and May of 1777 during the Revolutionary era. He settled in Blackville Township, Barnwell County, South Carolina, where he died in 1816.
LBB7-JN8: John Talbot was born in 1621 in Malahide, County Dublin, Ireland, where he held the title of Lord of Malahide. He lived and died in his native Malahide, passing away in 1672.
LTN5-34V: John Bass Sr. was born in 1673 in Nansemond, Virginia. He made his home in Bertie, North Carolina, where he lived for much of his life and died in 1732.
LD1B-53P: Henry Bramlett II was born in 1729 in Prince William, Virginia. He took up residence in Fauquier County by 1770 and died there in 1781.
KN77-JMP: Elinor Sayles was born in 1671 in Providence, Rhode Island. She lived in the colony of Rhode Island and died in 1714 in Warwick, in Providence County.
L643-VD6: Richard Austin III was born in 1632 in Titchfield, Hampshire, England. He emigrated to New England, where he was made a freeman in May 1671, and died in 1703 in Charlestown, Massachusetts Bay Colony.
K8NM-4F4: Hans Jacob Gerster was born in 1643 in Gelterkinden, in the Basel-Landschaft region of Switzerland. Records of baptisms in Switzerland, including one in February 1669 in his native Gelterkinden, mark his life there.
LY28-WL3: Henry Long Sr was born in 1650 in Old Rappahannock, Virginia. He lived in colonial Virginia and died in 1733 in Hanover Parish, King George County.
L2S5-HFS: John Catlett III was born in 1596 in Sittingbourne, Kent, England. He wrote his will in July 1625, and it was proved that October, the year of his death in his native Kent at around the age of twenty-nine.
L4JV-6P4: Thomas Hammond was born in 1668 in Anne Arundel, Maryland. He rose to the rank of Colonel in military service. He wrote his will in December 1724 and died that year in Baltimore, Maryland.
M5LN-9Y9: Mary Barnard was born in 1609 in Worksop, Nottinghamshire, England, and immigrated to Boston in February 1631. She moved to Weymouth in the Massachusetts Bay Colony in 1635 before settling in Providence, Rhode Island, the following year. She died in Providence in 1676.
LVC1-SGD: Joseph Phipps I was born in 1640 in Reading, Berkshire, England, where he was imprisoned as a Quaker dissenter for his faith. He immigrated to Pennsylvania in 1682 and the following year served as an assemblyman in Chester. He made his will in 1709 and died in 1716 in Abington, Montgomery Township, Philadelphia County.
LTLN-QMH: Benjamin Scott of Braintree was born in 1611 in England and worked as a tailor. He immigrated to New England around 1637 or 1638 and settled in Braintree, in the Massachusetts Bay Colony, where he died in 1647.
